text: In this video we will present to you two patients with cancer of four percent of the upper and lower part of the right and left side of the brain respectively, who suffered from hypertonic laparoscopic ovarian infarction.

The patient is positioned in a lateral fetal position. A trocar is inserted into the abdomen, followed by two 12mm trocars placed along the midline, one subcostal and the other anterior. A 10mm trocar is then placed midway between the two previously inserted trocars.

The surgeon begins by addressing the surfaces of the left kidney, specifically at the middle-to-bottom pole. The anatomical elements are recognized, and the circumference of the kidney is identified. The surgeon opens the line of Toldt, retracting the lower helix towards the midline. The dissection starts at the bottom pole of the kidney and extends downward, reaching the lagonia aegia. The left adductor muscle is pushed towards the midline, revealing the nerve gland and the anterior surface of the nerve. Emphasis is placed on the full mobilization of muscles on the anterior surface of the nerve for safe removal.
The surgeon prepares the surgical frames and positions them visibly on the side of the abdominal wall. The Ruhmeltourniquet technique is employed for the heat design phase of the procedure.

The surgeon releases pressure using xylitol on the surgical field, avoiding heat application. The perinephric fat is released along the joint, followed by internal pressure management using multiple V-Lock sutures.

After we locked the passage of the first frame with hemlock, we continue with the second frame v-lock until the completion of the internal printing. Then the thermal structure is made. The thermal structure time was of about fourteen minutes. An external nephrology is followed and the number one vikril frame is used with a non-zero four and the hemolog is used. After we have locked the passage of the first frame with the hemolog, we continue with the second frame, vikril number one, ...until the completion of the external nephrology.

The fully mobilized nephron is placed inside a collection bag at the corner of the silent surface of the upper city of the right nephron. The nerve is repositioned, and serotonin is circulated. The bronchitis is placed, followed by the removal of the trachea under visual guidance to control potential hemorrhage. The video shows the placement of the bronchitis after the left part of the nerve and the external view after closing the surgical site.
